title: IRAs
description: Practice understanding of IRAs: Traditional and Roth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Both Traditional and Roth IRA have taxes you have to pay at a specific time point.

**When do you pay taxes with a Roth IRA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when you retire and take the money out
when you initially put the money in

assessment_items.question_content:
IRAs are retirement accounts that provide tax advantages, but how and when you pay taxes can vary.

**What happens when you withdraw from a Traditional IRA in retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You pay taxes on everything you take out.
You pay taxes only on the money you earned in the account.
You pay taxes only on the money you put in.

assessment_items.question_content:
Choosing the right type of IRA often depends on your expectations about future taxes.

**Why might someone prefer a Roth IRA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They prefer to pay taxes on a smaller contribution than on a larger withdrawal.
They want a tax break right now.
They believe they won't ever have to pay taxes.
They expect their tax rate to decrease over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
You need to withdraw money from your Traditional IRA before reaching age 59½.

**What is the impact of this early withdrawal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you pay ordinary income tax and a $10\%$ penalty
you pay no taxes or penalties
you pay just ordinary income tax

assessment_items.question_content:
You have urgent expenses and need to access your Roth IRA funds before turning 59½.

**Which part can you take out without having to pay taxes or penalties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: contributions, up to what you originally put in
none, you always pay taxes and penalties

assessment_items.question_content:
You have a Traditional IRA while your partner has a Roth IRA. Recently, you had an emergency expense and have no choice but to withdraw early from an IRA.

**Whose IRA should you withdraw from to minimize taxes and penalties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: doesn't matter which IRA you choose

assessment_items.question_content:
When you deposit money into an IRA, it will grow and potentially double several times. 

**While the money *grows* inside an IRA, how is it taxed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: at your current income tax rate

assessment_items.question_content:
After you deposit money into a Roth IRA, it will grow and potentially double several times.

**While the money is *growing* inside a Roth IRA, how is it taxed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: at your current income tax rate

assessment_items.question_content:
After you deposit money into a Traditional IRA, it will grow and potentially double several times.

**While the money is *growing* inside a Traditional IRA, how is it taxed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: at your current income tax rate

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a table that shows a Traditional IRA you opened in your 20's. 

|| **Traditional IRA growth ** ||
**Description** | **Traditional IRA**
- | -
Initial contribution | $\$5{,}000$
Tax rate (while working) | $28\%$
Starting balance | $\$5{,}000$
First doubling | $\$10{,}000$
Second doubling | $\$20,000$
Third doubling | $\$40{,}000$
Retirement tax rate | $11\%$
Early withdrawal penalty | $10\%$

You are now $60$ years old, and decided to retire.

**If you start taking money out of your Traditional IRA, which (if any) penalties or taxes will you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a table that shows a Roth IRA you opened in your 20's. 

|| **Roth IRA growth ** ||
**Description** | **Roth IRA**
- | -
Initial contribution | $\$6{,}000$
Tax rate (while working) | $25\%$
Starting balance | $\$4{,}500$
First doubling | $\$9{,}000$
Second doubling | $\$18{,}000$
Third doubling | $\$36{,}000$
Retirement tax rate | N/A
Early withdrawal penalty | $10\%$

You are now $60$ years old, and decided to retire.

**If you start taking money out of your Roth IRA, which (if any) penalties or taxes will you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a table that shows a Traditional IRA you opened in your 20's. 

|| **Traditional IRA growth ** ||
**Description** | **Traditional IRA**
- | -
Initial contribution | $\$5{,}000$
Tax rate (while working) | $28\%$
Starting balance | $\$5{,}000$
First doubling | $\$10{,}000$
Second doubling | $\$20,000$
Third doubling | $\$40{,}000$
Retirement tax rate | $11\%$
Early withdrawal penalty | $10\%$

You are now $55$ years old, and have decided to retire.

**If you start taking money out of your Traditional IRA, which (if any) penalties or taxes will you have to pay?**
